channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Stop Writing Prompts. Start Writing Loops.
item.description: For over a year now most people have used LLMs the same way. Write a prompt, send it, get a response. If it is not good enough tweak the prompt and try again. If it works copy the result and move...

item.title: Why MCP Is Becoming the Standard Layer for AI Integrations (And Why Your Team Will Eventually Use It)
item.description: AI doesn't need another model. It needs a sane, reusable way to connect models to the real world: tools, data, APIs, and workflows. That's exactly the problem the Model Context Protocol (MCP) is...

item.title: When Cloud Agent Platforms Pull the Plug, Local Deployment Is the Only Foundation
item.description: Two of China's largest AI model platforms, Doubao and Qwen, both tightened restrictions on custom agents last week. A batch of third-party agents were delisted or had their distribution throttled,...

item.title: AI-Run Ransomware: The Autopilot Was Impressive, the Pilot Still Booked the Flight
item.description: The story matters, but maybe not for the reason the headline wants you to think "First AI-run ransomware attack" is a hell of a sentence to put in a headline, and it's already doing the rounds...

item.title: I built a screen recorder that turns raw captures into finished video stories — no install, no backend
item.description: I built a screen recorder that turns raw captures into finished video stories — no install, no backend Every product demo I recorded looked the same: a raw screen capture, no cursor highlights, no...

item.title: 99.9% Uptime: How Much Downtime Is That, Really? (Plus Combined SLA)
item.description: "99.9% uptime" sounds like a small, almost meaningless gap from perfect. In practice it's the difference between an outage nobody notices and one that ends up in a postmortem. Whether you're...

item.title: How to Generate TypeScript Types from JSON (Without Getting Optional Fields Wrong)
item.description: If you've ever pasted a single JSON object into a "json to typescript" tool and gotten back a clean interface, only to have your API return a slightly different shape the next day and blow up your...

item.title: HTTP client as a first-class citizen: zero deps, bit-for-bit parity, async by default
item.description: Webhooks, scraping, health checks, proxying. Every language solves outbound HTTP with an external library. In Fitz, http.get/post/put/delete/head/request are language builtins. Async from day one....

item.title: HTTP client outbound como ciudadano de primera clase: cero deps, paridad bit-a-bit, async natural
item.description: Webhooks, scraping, health checks, proxying. Cada lenguaje resuelve HTTP client outbound con una librería externa. En Fitz, http.get/post/put/delete/head/request son builtins del lenguaje. Async...

item.title: Client Off‑Boarding Blueprint: End Bad Projects Gracefully
item.description: Client Off‑Boarding Blueprint: End Bad Projects Gracefully 1. The myth you’re probably living by – “just disappear and hope they don’t notice” When I started out, my biggest fear was the...

item.title: This Former DeepMind Exec Thinks the AI Arms Race Could End in Disaster
item.description: Verity Harding tells WIRED that the US government’s nationalistic attitude toward AI is evidence that a worst-case scenario is taking shape.

item.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
item.description: A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices. According...

item.title: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
item.description: Samsung has announced that its next Galaxy Unpacked launch event will be held on July 22nd, with the tagline: "A new shape unfolds." It's long been rumored that Samsung is about to expand its...

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
item.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

item.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
item.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."

item.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
item.description: Researchers at Nebula Security have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full root control of a machine that has not been...

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Actively Exploited Adobe, Joomla, and Langflow Flaws to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added four security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation....

item.title: Meta’s glasses will turn off the camera if you tamper with the privacy light
item.description: Amid public backlash over its smart glasses, Meta announced that it will be updating its glasses with a new feature that will disable the camera when it detects that someone has tampered with or...

item.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
item.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

item.title: Meta Now Lets Anyone Use Your Instagram Photos in AI Images—Unless You Opt Out
item.description: As part of Meta’s Muse Image model rollout, Instagram users with public accounts need to opt out to block AI generations of their content.

item.title: Of course viewers are giving up on Netflix shows
item.description: Even though Netflix is the world's most popular paid streaming service, the company has been struggling to keep viewers watching its series after their first seasons. Beef - the streamer's...

item.title: Netflix is about to host videos from BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, and other publishers
item.description: Starting on August 3rd, Netflix's streaming library will include video content from dozens of digital media brands including BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, Hearst Magazines, People Inc, and Tastemade. As...

item.title: OpenAI’s Chief Futurist Is Leaving the Company
item.description: Joshua Achiam spent nearly nine years at OpenAI researching AI safety and made a memorable appearance in the Musk v. Altman trial.

item.title: From Hugging Face to Amazon SageMaker Studio in one click
Meta’s new Muse Image model can pull other Instagram users into AI photos
item.description: Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and...

item.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
item.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: X says top accounts steal videos from other users as it announces new video tools
item.description: Nikita Bier, X's head of product, said in a post on Monday that "[m]any videos from top accounts are simply stolen from other users, sometimes 5 years after they originally went viral," while...
